strategy: Never hit Pendragon with a spell costing more than 3 pips. He answers with a 1,000 Death Scarecrow drain that heals him half, and killing him with a 4+ pip spell just heals him. Finish him with sub-4-pip hits or wands.

Kill the Black Dragon and White Dragon first. Black punishes every single-target spell and White punishes every AoE, both stripping your effects and self-healing on damage. Aegis and Indemnity do not protect against these removals.
Against the Black Dragon use multi-target spells; against the White Dragon use single-target spells. Each dragon ignores the other's kind of spell.
Avoid Heals near them (Black answers with Celestial Calendar, White with Frost Giant) and avoid DoTs (Triaged, and they trigger the dragon's self-heal).
Do not join late (Medusa). Pendragon resists Death 80% and takes +Life, so hit off-school; clear both dragons, then chip Pendragon with sub-4-pip hits.
Team: assign one wizard to the Black Dragon with AoEs and one to the White Dragon with single-target hits, then everyone finishes Pendragon cheaply.
cheatNotes: Pendragon punishes any damage spell that costs more than 3 pips.
A 4+ pip hit on him triggers a Scarecrow dealing 1,000 Death drain and healing him half of it.
Killing him with a 4+ pip spell makes him heal himself and Scarecrow anyway, so he survives.
A late-joining wizard gets hit with Medusa.
The Black Dragon punishes single-target spells.
Any single-target effect makes him cast a Shadow removal that ignores your protection.
A damage spell that also places an effect hits the target once and the caster twice with that removal (e.g. Volcanic Golem: single Pierce on target, double on caster).
A damage spell that heals the target, like Link, keeps its HoT but still triggers the single-target damage cheat on the wizard.
A single-target Blade triggers a Disarm removing 1-3 blades based on how many were cast.
Globals count and trigger a single Disarm.
A single-target Ward triggers a Pierce removing 1-3 wards.
A single-target HoT triggers a Remove HOT clearing 1-3 heal-over-times.
Casting a Heal on a wizard makes the Black Dragon hit that wizard with Celestial Calendar.
Heal-over-Times count as heals for that cheat.
A single-target DoT on the Black Dragon triggers a Triage removing 1-3 DoTs.
That DoT removal also triggers his single-target damage self-heal below.
A single-target damage spell on the Black Dragon makes him cast Blessing and heal to full.
Traps on the Black Dragon trigger a Cleanse Ward removing 1-3 negative traps.
Prisms do not trigger the trap removal.
Negative Charms on the Black Dragon trigger a Cleanse Charm removing 1-3 of them.
Aegis and Indemnity cannot shield any of these dragon removals.
The White Dragon punishes multi-target spells.
Any multi-target effect makes him cast a Shadow removal that ignores your protection.
A multi-target Blade triggers an AoE Disarm removing 1-2 blades from all wizards.
A multi-target Shield triggers an AoE Pierce removing 1-2 wards from all wizards.
A multi-target HoT triggers an AoE Remove HOT clearing 1-2 heal-over-times from all wizards.
A multi-target Heal makes the White Dragon cast Frost Giant (530).
A multi-target DoT on the White Dragon triggers a Triage removing 1-3 DoTs.
That removal also triggers his multi-target damage self-heal below.
A multi-target damage spell on the White Dragon makes him cast Blessing and heal to full.
A multi-target Trap on the White Dragon triggers a Cleanse Ward removing 1-3 negative traps.
A multi-target negative Charm on the White Dragon triggers a Cleanse Charm removing 1-3 of them.
